steps.title: Use a restrictive clause
steps.content: The sentence needs a subject that names which materials highlight Palma Rodríguez's farming and engineering background. The words after materials form a restrictive that-clause because they identify the specific materials being discussed.

steps.title: Keep the subject unbroken
steps.content: A puts a comma before that, incorrectly treating the identifying clause as extra information. C adds a comma before the verb highlight, splitting the subject from its predicate. D makes both comma errors. The no-comma version keeps the noun and restrictive clause together. The answer is B.
